152: Statics
153: -------
154: 
155: .. rubric:: Syntax
156: 
157: .. syntax::
158:

164: 
165: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
166: 
167: :dp:`fls_ibrmiwfypldh`
168: A :dt:`static` is a :t:`value` that is associated with a specific memory
169: location.
170: 
171: :dp:`fls_mt94jvoot9dx`
172: A :t:`static` defined within a :t:`generic function` exists once in the
173: output executable or library.
174: 
175: :dp:`fls_k0r2c6uq29tu`
176: The :t:`type specification` of a :t:`static` shall have ``'static``
177: :t:`lifetime`.
178: 
179: :dp:`fls_b6ods85htuyn`
180: The :t:`type` of a :t:`static` shall implement the :std:`core::marker::Sized`
181: :t:`trait`.
182: 
183: :dp:`fls_WRpcVF1fLEpr`
184: A :t:`static` shall only be subject to an :s:`ItemSafety` if it is an :t:`external static` in an :t:`unsafe external block`.
185: 
186: :dp:`fls_doi4z6u55bi7`
187: A :t:`mutable static` is a :t:`static` with :t:`keyword` ``mut`` whose
188: :t:`value` can be modified.
189: 
190: :dp:`fls_74hp208pto22`
191: Access to a :t:`mutable static` shall require :t:`unsafe context`.
192: 
193: :dp:`fls_jfde2vg6mtww`
194: An :dt:`immutable static` is a :t:`static` whose :t:`value` cannot be modified.
195: 
196: :dp:`fls_k4tyqb1j6zjo`
197: The type of an :t:`immutable static` shall implement the
198: :std:`core::marker::Sync` :t:`trait`.
199: 
200: :dp:`fls_t17h5h6a6v4c`
201: A :t:`static initializer` is a :t:`construct` that provides the :t:`value` of
202: its related :t:`static`.
203: 
204: :dp:`fls_yq0hpy4jx2qb`
205: A :t:`static` shall have a :t:`static initializer`, unless it is an
206: :t:`external static`.
207: 
208: :dp:`fls_vgidvfwzm4ks`
209: The :t:`expression` of a :t:`static initializer` shall be a
210: :t:`constant expression`.
211: 
212: :dp:`fls_8dcldbvu7lav`
213: A use of a :t:`static` is a :t:`place expression` referring to the unique
214: location of the :t:`static`.
215: 
216: .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
217: 
218: :dp:`fls_w0nb0mphho7b`
219: The :t:`elaboration` of a :t:`static` evaluates its :t:`static initializer`.
220: 
221: :dp:`fls_eeocxst9vafn`
222: All :t:`[path]s` that refer to a :t:`static` refer to the same memory location.
223: 
224: :dp:`fls_47khd5ljsxeq`
225: A :t:`static` is not :t:`dropped` during :t:`destruction`.
226: 
227: :dp:`fls_dowxbphqvk3n`
228: An :t:`immutable static` whose :t:`type` is not subject to
229: :t:`interior mutability` may reside in read-only memory.
230:

233: :dp:`fls_b5wsmii7vz3v`
234: It is undefined behavior to mutate an :t:`immutable static` whose :t:`type` is

403: * :dp:`fls_pdDr8Lk1GQ0T`
404:   Any :t:`expression` whose :t:`evaluation` requires the :t:`evaluation` of a
405:   diverging :t:`subexpression` on all reachable control flow paths.
406:

408: 
409: Place Expressions
410: ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
411: 
412: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
413: 
414: :dp:`fls_yBEzAdfSAMYQ`
415: A :dt:`place` is a location where a :t:`value` resides.
416: 
417: :dp:`fls_qbrcg3cl9td`
418: A :dt:`place expression` is an :t:`expression` that represents a memory
419: location. The following :t:`[expression]s` are :t:`[place expression]s`:
420: 
421: * :dp:`fls_jpmhibm4omm7`
422:   :t:`[Dereference expression]s`,
423: 
424: * :dp:`fls_none1dykbn8c`
425:   :t:`[Field access expression]s`,
426: 
427: * :dp:`fls_lj7x5dgbmg9i`
428:   :t:`[Index expression]s`,
429: 
430: * :dp:`fls_anzidgx02lly`
431:   :t:`[Parenthesized expression]s` where the :t:`operand` is a
432:   :t:`place expression`,
433: 
434: * :dp:`fls_ya05djl1d154`
435:   :t:`[Path expression]s` that resolve to a :t:`static` or a :t:`variable`.
436: 
437: * :dp:`fls_gv4M0DE3OMwk`
438:   A :dt:`temporary`.
439: 
440: :dp:`fls_ku38h562vfyl`
441: A :dt:`mutable place expression` is a :t:`place expression` whose memory
442: location can be modified. The following :t:`[place expression]s` are
443: :t:`[mutable place expression]s`:
444: 
445: :dp:`fls_wb006containerop`
446: A :dt:`container operand` is an :t:`operand` that indicates the :t:`value`
447: whose :t:`field` is selected in a :t:`field access expression`.
448: 
449: * :dp:`fls_1tq2o2huda9l`
450:   A :t:`dereference expression` whose :t:`operand`'s :t:`type` implements the
451:   :std:`core::ops::DerefMut` :t:`trait`,
452: 
453: * :dp:`fls_6b4rwkrc1ap6`
454:   A :t:`dereference expression` whose :t:`operand`'s :t:`type` is a
455:   :t:`mutable raw pointer type` (a :t:`raw pointer type` subject to
456:   :t:`keyword` ``mut``),
457: 
458: * :dp:`fls_s4bhrpykzmm7`
459:   A :t:`dereference expression` whose :t:`operand`'s :t:`type` is a
460:   :t:`mutable reference type` (a :t:`reference type` subject to :t:`keyword`
461:   ``mut``),
462: 
463: * :dp:`fls_xm0gm2q27x2e`
464:   A :t:`field access expression` where the :t:`container operand` is a
465:   :t:`mutable place expression`,
466: 
467: * :dp:`fls_bt50fltfqcvn`
468:   An :dt:`index expression` whose :t:`type` implements the
469:   :std:`core::ops::IndexMut` :t:`trait`,
470: 
471: * :dp:`fls_Sgfxp186GMvz`
472:   :t:`[Parenthesized expression]s` where the :t:`operand` is a
473:   :t:`mutable place expression`,
474: 
475: * :dp:`fls_ilaqmj3hc5uv`
476:   A :t:`path expression` that resolves to a :dt:`mutable static`,
477: 
478: * :dp:`fls_m0gbw9myylv2`
479:   A :t:`path expression` that resolves to a :t:`mutable variable` that is not
480:   currently borrowed,
481: 
482: * :dp:`fls_dcm3yr3y9y0a`
483:   A :t:`temporary`.
484: 
485: :dp:`fls_cPEMHZtPkctX`
486: An :dt:`immutable place expression` is a :t:`place expression` whose memory
487: location cannot be modified. All :t:`[place expression]s` that are not
488: :t:`[mutable place expression]s` are :t:`[immutable place expression]s`.
489: 
490: :dp:`fls_4vxi1ji93dxb`
491: A :dt:`place expression context` is a :t:`construct` that may evaluate its
492: :t:`operand` as a memory location.
493: 
494: :dp:`fls_fzsrdrHnndRd`
495: The following :t:`[construct]s` are :t:`[place expression context]s`:
496: 
497: * :dp:`fls_ZED5wJQVO6nf`
498:   The :t:`assignee operand` of an :t:`assignment expression` or a
499:   :t:`compound assignment expression`,
500: 
501: * :dp:`fls_Ufz9W5vyZkv3`
502:   The :t:`operand` of a :t:`borrow expression`,
503: 
504: * :dp:`fls_K7SbApHPmwjM`
505:   The :t:`operand` of a :t:`raw borrow expression`,
506: 
507: * :dp:`fls_KxWIzoh9WwK7`
508:   The :t:`operand` of a :t:`dereference expression`,
509: 
510: * :dp:`fls_oV9Hd6OiFAZX`
511:   The :t:`container operand` of a :t:`field access expression`,
512: 
513: * :dp:`fls_NnGiNsH6Zgmp`
514:   The initialization :t:`expression` of a :t:`let statement`,
515: 
516: * :dp:`fls_jLZlxIHr4w2v`
517:   The :t:`operand` of an :dt:`implicit borrow`,
518: 
519: * :dp:`fls_giZ7w1G02JSg`
520:   The :dt:`indexed operand` of an :t:`index expression`,
521: 
522: * :dp:`fls_5yXuTLQOQ3cc`
523:   The :t:`subject let expression` of an :t:`if let expression` or a
524:   :t:`while let loop expression`,
525: 
526: * :dp:`fls_nman7mJVSQlm`
527:   The :t:`subject expression` of a :t:`match expression`,
528: 
529: * :dp:`fls_JBfZuFDQg3mU`
530:   The :t:`base initializer` of a :dt:`struct expression`.
531: 
532: :dp:`fls_wxGAOWEVT77u`
533: A :dt:`mutable place expression context` is a :t:`place expression context` that
534: may evaluate its :t:`operand` as a mutable memory location. The following
535: :t:`[construct]s` are :t:`[mutable place expression context]s`:
536: 
537: * :dp:`fls_qytgkbhqr5ln`
538:   The :t:`indexed operand` of an :t:`index expression` if evaluated in a
539:   :t:`mutable place expression context`,
540: 
541: * :dp:`fls_5gy92rsi2mqm`
542:   The :t:`assignee operand` of an :t:`assignment expression` or a
543:   :t:`compound assignment expression`,
544: 
545: * :dp:`fls_u80htrnr2ebz`
546:   The :t:`operand` of a mutable :t:`borrow expression`,
547: 
548: * :dp:`fls_o0feajus3jtu`
549:   The :t:`operand` of a :t:`dereference expression` if evaluated in a

551: 
552: * :dp:`fls_ffjx1d5dseo4`
553:   The :t:`container operand` of :t:`field access expression` if evaluated in a
554:   :t:`mutable place expression context`,
555: 
556: * :dp:`fls_9r7dopqf1nzl`
557:   The :t:`subject let expression` of an :t:`if let expression` or a

570: An :dt:`immutable place expression context` is a :t:`place expression context`

3478: Invocation Expressions
3479: ----------------------
3480: 
3481: .. _fls_xa4nbfas01cj:

3483: Call Expressions
3484: ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
3485: 
3486: .. rubric:: Syntax
3487: 
3488: .. syntax::
3489: 
3490:    CallExpression ::=
3491:        CallOperand $$($$ ArgumentOperandList? $$)$$
3492: 
3493:    CallOperand ::=
3494:        Operand
3495: 
3496:    ArgumentOperandList ::=
3497:        ExpressionList
3498: 
3499: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
3500: 
3501: :dp:`fls_fvgfx17ossd9`
3502: A :dt:`call expression` is an :t:`expression` that invokes a :t:`function` or
3503: constructs a :dt:`tuple enum variant value` or a :dt:`tuple struct value`.
3504: 
3505: :dp:`fls_v1q7n3t5c9xz`
3506: A :t:`tuple enum variant` is an :t:`enum variant` with a
3507: :s:`TupleStructFieldList`.
3508: 
3509: :dp:`fls_q1m7v9b3k5ha`
3510: A :dt:`tuple struct` is a :t:`struct` with a :s:`TupleStructFieldList`.
3511: 
3512: :dp:`fls_g6t2n8c5p4xy`
3513: A :dt:`tuple struct type` is the :t:`type` of a :t:`tuple struct`.
3514: 
3515: :dp:`fls_jvz5z3eqxb39`
3516: An :dt:`argument operand` is an :t:`operand` which is used as an argument in a
3517: :t:`call expression` or a :t:`method call expression`.
3518: 
3519: :dp:`fls_m8n4t5c1k0pz`
3520: A :dt:`tuple` is a :t:`value` that wraps :t:`[argument operand]s` in
3521: :t:`function trait` calls.
3522: 
3523: :dp:`fls_7ql1c71eidg8`
3524: A :dt:`call operand` is the :t:`function` being invoked or the
3525: :t:`tuple enum variant value` or the :t:`tuple struct value` being constructed
3526: by a :t:`call expression`.
3527: 
3528: :dp:`fls_qOTBw10dW37D`
3529: An :dt:`adjusted call operand` is the :t:`call operand` after any implicit
3530: adjustments required by :t:`call resolution`.
3531: 
3532: :dp:`fls_4t6imtiw6kzt`
3533: A :dt:`callee type` is either a :t:`function item type`, a
3534: :t:`function pointer type`, a :t:`tuple enum variant`, a
3535: :t:`tuple struct type`, or a :t:`type` that implements any of the
3536: :std:`core::ops::Fn`, :std:`core::ops::FnMut`, or :std:`core::ops::FnOnce`
3537: :t:`[trait]s`.
3538: 
3539: :dp:`fls_liwnzwu1el1i`
3540: An :dt:`unsafe function item type` is a :t:`function item type` where the related
3541: :t:`function` is an :t:`unsafe function`.
3542: 
3543: :dp:`fls_5dd7icjcl3nt`
3544: An :dt:`unsafe function pointer type` is a :t:`function pointer type` subject to
3545: :t:`keyword` ``unsafe``.
3546: 
3547: :dp:`fls_bu6i3mcvnbin`
3548: The :t:`type` of a :t:`call expression` is the :t:`return type` of the invoked
3549: :t:`function`, the :t:`type` of the :t:`tuple enum variant` or the
3550: :t:`tuple struct` being constructed, or :t:`associated type`
3551: :std:`core::ops::FnOnce::Output`.
3552: 
3553: :dp:`fls_8ljrgdept7s8`
3554: A :t:`call expression` whose :t:`callee type` is either an
3555: :t:`external function item type`, an :t:`unsafe function item type`, or an
3556: :t:`unsafe function pointer type` shall require :t:`unsafe context`.
3557: 
3558: :dp:`fls_7p6zrjbpj0kl`
3559: The :t:`value` of a :t:`call expression` is determined as follows:
3560: 
3561: * :dp:`fls_yrr1s0tucgvh`
3562:   If the :t:`callee type` is a :t:`function item type` or a
3563:   :t:`function pointer type`, then the :t:`value` is the result of invoking the
3564:   corresponding :t:`function` with the :t:`[argument operand]s`.
3565: 
3566: * :dp:`fls_RZjFs9koNOk8`
3567:   If the :t:`callee type` is a :t:`tuple enum variant` or a
3568:   :t:`tuple struct type`, then the :t:`value` is the result of constructing
3569:   the :t:`tuple enum variant` or the :t:`tuple struct` with the
3570:   :t:`[argument operand]s`.
3571: 
3572: * :dp:`fls_s3q3sej1hgho`
3573:   If the :t:`callee type` implements the :std:`core::ops::Fn` :t:`trait`, then
3574:   the :t:`value` is the result of invoking
3575:   ``core::ops::Fn::call(adjusted_call_operand, argument_operand_tuple)``,
3576:   where ``adjusted_call_operand`` is the :t:`adjusted call operand`, and
3577:   ``argument_operand_tuple`` is a :t:`tuple` that wraps the
3578:   :t:`[argument operand]s`.
3579: 
3580: * :dp:`fls_cu2ubdm3tfwb`
3581:   If the :t:`call operand` implements the :std:`core::ops::FnMut` :t:`trait`,
3582:   then the :t:`value` is the result of invoking
3583:   ``core::ops::FnMut::call_mut(adjusted_call_operand, argument_operand_tuple),``
3584:   where ``adjusted_call_operand`` is the :t:`adjusted call operand`, and
3585:   ``argument_operand_tuple`` is a :t:`tuple` that wraps the
3586:   :t:`[argument operand]s`.
3587: 
3588: * :dp:`fls_9bbewx1l7h5h`
3589:   If the :t:`call operand` implements the :std:`core::ops::FnOnce` :t:`trait`,
3590:   then the :t:`value` is the result of invoking
3591:   ``core::ops::FnOnce::call_once(adjusted_call_operand, argument_operand_tuple),``
3592:   where ``adjusted_call_operand`` is the :t:`adjusted call operand`, and
3593:   ``argument_operand_tuple`` is a :t:`tuple` that wraps the
3594:   :t:`[argument operand]s`.
3595: 
3596: :dp:`fls_ZSkcro52q097`
3597: A :t:`call expression` is subject to :t:`call resolution`.
3598: 
3599: .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
3600: 
3601: :dp:`fls_ggr5i91vur0r`
3602: The :t:`evaluation` of a :t:`call expression` proceeds as follows:
3603: 
3604: #. :dp:`fls_hwalzgdidbfz`
3605:    The :t:`call operand` is evaluated.
3606: 
3607: #. :dp:`fls_p52mfvpadu7w`
3608:    The :t:`[argument operand]s` are evaluated in left-to-right order.
3609: 
3610: #. :dp:`fls_1cyo5qhbl1j9`
3611:    If the :t:`adjusted call operand` is a :t:`function item type` or
3612:    :t:`function pointer type`, then corresponding :t:`function` is invoked.
3613: 
3614: #. :dp:`fls_nb0eqky2akzt`
3615:    If the :t:`type` of the :t:`call operand` implements the
3616:    :std:`core::ops::Fn` :t:`trait`, then
3617:    ``core::ops::Fn::call(adjusted_call_operand, argument_operand_tuple)`` is
3618:    invoked.
3619: 
3620: #. :dp:`fls_9lt4wh9ql5ae`
3621:    If the :t:`type` of the :t:`call operand` implements the
3622:    :std:`core::ops::FnMut` :t:`trait`, then
3623:    ``core::ops::FnMut::call_mut(adjusted_call_operand, argument_operand_tuple)``
3624:    is invoked.
3625: 
3626: #. :dp:`fls_ixebnlcccmit`
3627:    If the :t:`type` of the :t:`call operand` implements the
3628:    :std:`core::ops::FnOnce` :t:`trait`, then
3629:    ``core::ops::FnOnce::call_once(adjusted_call_operand, argument_operand_tuple)``
3630:    is invoked.
3631: 
3632: .. rubric:: Undefined Behavior
3633: 
3634: :dp:`fls_PIR4LRdYSKjj`
3635: A :dt:`call conformance` is the requirement that a :t:`call expression` uses
3636: the :t:`ABI` of the invoked :t:`function`.
3637: 
3638: :dp:`fls_5yeq4oah58dl`
3639: It is undefined behavior to call a :t:`function` with an :t:`ABI` other than the
3640: :t:`ABI` the :t:`function` was defined with.
3641:

3643:

3645:

3650: Method Call Expressions
3651: ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
3652: 
3653: .. rubric:: Syntax
3654: 
3655: .. syntax::
3656: 
3657:    MethodCallExpression ::=
3658:        ReceiverOperand $$.$$ MethodOperand $$($$ ArgumentOperandList? $$)$$
3659: 
3660:    ReceiverOperand ::=

3668: :dp:`fls_b7i26954j1hc`

3670: of a :t:`variable`.
3671: 
3672: :dp:`fls_jx3ryre0xs88`
3673: A :dt:`receiver operand` is an :t:`operand` that denotes the :t:`value` whose
3674: :t:`method` is being invoked by a :t:`method call expression`.
3675: 
3676: :dp:`fls_3AQUOBo7akXu`
3677: A :dt:`method operand` is an :t:`operand` that denotes the :t:`method` being
3678: invoked by a :t:`method call expression`.
3679: 
3680: :dp:`fls_11glzggtbgb3`
3681: The :t:`type` of a :t:`method call expression` is the :t:`return type` of the
3682: invoked :t:`method`.
3683: 
3684: :dp:`fls_ljvj1f9fv085`
3685: The :t:`value` of a :t:`method call expression` is the :t:`value` returned by
3686: the invoked :t:`method`.
3687: 
3688: :dp:`fls_y7bj7y6davlh`
3689: A :t:`method call expression` is subject to :t:`method resolution`.
3690: 
3691: .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
3692: 
3693: :dp:`fls_oxxk3snd7ya0`
3694: The :t:`evaluation` of a :t:`method call expression` proceeds as follows:
3695: 
3696: #. :dp:`fls_gmpq15g77o20`
3697:    The :t:`receiver operand` is evaluated.
3698: 
3699: #. :dp:`fls_pu0n9hakkym2`
3700:    The :t:`[argument operand]s` are evaluated in left-to-right order.
3701: 
3702: #. :dp:`fls_cawdkgvvd1x6`
3703:    The :t:`method` is invoked.

4001: 
4002: :dp:`fls_BjZjuiFnPtFd`
4003: A :dt:`loop body` is the :t:`block expression` of a :t:`loop expression`.
4004: 
4005: :dp:`fls_XEc0cIkpkyzJ`
4006: The :t:`type` of the :t:`loop body` shall be the :t:`unit type`.
4007: 
4008: :dp:`fls_1MTa81vtePN8`
4009: A :dt:`label` is the :t:`name` of a :t:`loop expression` or a
4010: :t:`named block expression`.
4011: 
4012: :dp:`fls_eg93m93gvwal`
4013: An :dt:`anonymous loop expression` is a :t:`loop expression` without a
4014: :t:`label`.
4015: 
4016: :dp:`fls_phpoq9ho8f1v`
4017: A :dt:`named loop expression` is a :t:`loop expression` with a :t:`label`.
4018: 
4019: .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
4020: 
4021: :dp:`fls_aw6qczl4zpko`
4022: A :t:`loop expression` is :dt:`terminated` when its :t:`block expression` is no
4023: longer evaluated.
4024: 
4025: .. _fls_onfyolkcbeh3:
4026: 
4027: For Loops
4028: ~~~~~~~~~
4029: 
4030: .. rubric:: Syntax
4031: 
4032: .. syntax::
4033: 
4034:    ForLoopExpression ::=
4035:        $$for$$ Pattern $$in$$ SubjectExpression LoopBody
4036: 
4037: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
4038: 
4039: :dp:`fls_RPflON3LZDnO`
4040: For :dt:`for loop`, see :t:`for loop expression`.
4041: 
4042: :dp:`fls_1bh2alh37frz`
4043: A :dt:`for loop expression` is a :t:`loop expression` that continues to evaluate
4044: its :t:`loop body` as long as its :t:`subject expression` yields a :t:`value`.
4045: 
4046: :dp:`fls_fkgbin6ydkm4`
4047: The :t:`type` of a :t:`subject expression` shall implement the
4048: :std:`core::iter::IntoIterator` :t:`trait`.
4049: 
4050: :dp:`fls_fo6Aa6Td6rMA`
4051: The :t:`expected type` of the :t:`pattern` is the :t:`associated type` :std:`core::iter::IntoIterator::Item` of the :t:`subject expression`'s :std:`core::iter::IntoIterator` implementation.
4052: 
4053: :dp:`fls_bmTjhKdpfgCB`
4054: The :t:`type` of a :t:`for loop expression` is the :t:`unit type`.
4055: 
4056: :dp:`fls_FkxLf91WKiIo`
4057: The :t:`value` of a :t:`for loop expression` is the :t:`unit value`.
4058: 
4059: .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
4060: 
4061: :dp:`fls_kuxo0on3vit6`
4062: The :t:`evaluation` of a :t:`for loop expression` of the form
4063: 
4064: .. code-block:: rust
4065: 
4066:    'label: for pattern in subject_expression {
4067:        /* loop body */
4068:    }
4069: 
4070: :dp:`fls_2lrzrtjhsdes`
4071: is equivalent to the :t:`evaluation` of the following :t:`block expression`:
4072: 
4073: .. code-block:: rust
4074: 
4075:    {
4076:        let result =
4077:            match core::iter::IntoIterator::into_iter
4078:                (subject_expression)
4079:        {
4080:            mut iter => 'label: loop {
4081:                let mut next_value;
4082:                match core::iter::Iterator::next(&mut iter) {
4083:                    Option::Some(value) => next_value = value,
4084:                    Option::None => break
4085:                };
4086:                let pattern = next_value;
4087:                let () = { /* loop body */ };
4088:            }
4089:        };
4090:        result
4091:    }
4092: 
4093: .. rubric:: Examples
4094: 
4095: .. code-block:: rust
4096: 
4097:    let favorite_fruits = &["apples", "pears", "strawberries"];
4098: 
4099:    for fruit in favorite_fruits {
4100:        println!("I like eating {}.", fruit);
4101:    }
4102: 
4103: .. _fls_sf4qnd43z2wc:
4104: 
4105: Infinite Loops
4106: ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
4107: 
4108: .. rubric:: Syntax
4109: 
4110: .. syntax::
4111: 
4112:    InfiniteLoopExpression ::=
4113:        $$loop$$ LoopBody
4114: 
4115: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
4116: 
4117: :dp:`fls_OcNtRVV2WaUe`
4118: For :dt:`infinite loop`, see :t:`infinite loop expression`.
4119: 
4120: :dp:`fls_p11qw6mtxlda`
4121: An :dt:`infinite loop expression` is a :t:`loop expression` that continues to
4122: evaluate its :t:`loop body` indefinitely.
4123: 
4124: :dp:`fls_b314wjbv0zwe`
4125: The :t:`type` of an :t:`infinite loop expression` is determined as follows:
4126: 
4127: * :dp:`fls_rpedapxnv8w3`
4128:   If the :t:`infinite loop expression` does not contain a :t:`break expression`,
4129:   then the :t:`type` is the :t:`never type`.
4130: 
4131: * :dp:`fls_wf11yp1jwf53`
4132:   If the :t:`infinite loop expression` contains at least one
4133:   :t:`break expression`, then the :t:`type` is the :t:`unified type` of the
4134:   :t:`[break type]s` of all :t:`[break expression]s`.
4135: 
4136: :dp:`fls_q3qpcf2fz7h`
4137: The :t:`value` of an :t:`infinite loop expression` is determined as follows:
4138: 
4139: * :dp:`fls_2ulbzmuuny3g`
4140:   If the :t:`infinite loop expression` does not contain a :t:`break expression`,
4141:   then the :t:`value` is the :t:`unit value`.
4142: 
4143: * :dp:`fls_99imks9hj3kp`
4144:   If the :t:`infinite loop expression` contains at least one
4145:   :t:`break expression`, then the :t:`value` is the :t:`break value` of the
4146:   :t:`break expression` that broke out of the :t:`loop expression`.
4147: 
4148: .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
4149: 

(File has more lines. Use 'offset' parameter to read beyond line 4149)
</content>

<content>5000: :dp:`fls_u7jk4j8gkho`
5001: A :dt:`return expression` is an :t:`expression` that optionally yields a
5002: :t:`value` and causes control flow to return to the end of the enclosing
5003: :t:`control flow boundary`.
5004: 
5005: :dp:`fls_5v3j5ghhw8j8`
5006: A :t:`return expression` shall appear within a :t:`control flow boundary`.
5007: 
5008: :dp:`fls_7Ck4LMQMeQCv`
5009: The :t:`type` of a :t:`return expression` is the :t:`never type`.
5010: 
5011: :dp:`fls_r610t5vsi7bx`
5012: The :t:`value` returned by a :t:`return expression` is determined as follows:
5013: 
5014: * :dp:`fls_njndlx2rps2k`
5015:   If the :t:`return expression` has an :t:`operand`, then the :t:`value` is the
5016:   :t:`value` of the :t:`operand`.
5017: 
5018: * :dp:`fls_tjksia7prao1`
5019:   If the :t:`return expression` does not have an :t:`operand`, then the
5020:   :t:`value` is the :t:`unit value`.
5021: 
5022: .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
5023: 
5024: :dp:`fls_bqmwlona6l5w`
5025: The :t:`evaluation` of a :t:`return expression` proceeds as follows:
5026: 
5027: #. :dp:`fls_d9avvfi548t7`
5028:    If the :t:`return expression` has an :t:`operand`, then
5029: 
5030:    #. :dp:`fls_o3fc1z2mn8zc`
5031:       The :t:`operand` is evaluated.
5032: 
5033:    #. :dp:`fls_bbf54ukld7j9`
5034:       The :t:`value` of the :t:`operand` is :t:`passed <passing convention>`
5035:       :t:`by move` into the designated output location of the enclosing
5036:       :t:`control flow boundary`.
5037: 
5038: #. :dp:`fls_99ea30a5mulj`
5039:    Control destroys the current activation frame.
5040: 
5041: #. :dp:`fls_ubwj8uj6sbgt`
5042:    Control is transferred to the caller frame.
5043: 
5044: .. rubric:: Examples
5045: 
5046: .. code-block:: rust
5047: 
5048:    fn max(left: i32, right: i32) -> i32 {
5049:        if left > right {
5050:            return left;
5051:        }
5052:        return right;
5053:    }
5054: 
5055: .. _fls_hyrbmfmf6r8g:
5056: 
5057: Await Expressions
5058: -----------------
5059: 
5060: .. rubric:: Syntax
5061: 
5062: .. syntax::
5063: 
5064:    AwaitExpression ::=
5065:        FutureOperand $$.$$ $$await$$
5066: 
5067:    FutureOperand ::=
5068:        Operand
5069: 
5070: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
5071: 
5072: :dp:`fls_sjz5s71hwm7l`
5073: An :dt:`await expression` is an :t:`expression` that polls a :t:`future`,
5074: suspending the :t:`execution` of the :dt:`future` until the :t:`future` is ready.
5075: 
5076: :dp:`fls_vhchgab59jvd`
5077: A :dt:`future operand` is an :t:`operand` whose :t:`future` is being awaited by
5078: an :t:`await expression`.
5079: 
5080: :dp:`fls_k9pncajmhgk1`
5081: An :t:`await expression` shall appear within an
5082: :t:`async control flow boundary`. Only the innermost :t:`control flow boundary`
5083: shall be considered.
5084: 
5085: :dp:`fls_9uw5pd7kbrx3`
5086: The :t:`type` of a :t:`future operand` shall implement the
5087: :std:`core::future::IntoFuture` :t:`trait`.
5088: 
5089: :dp:`fls_c6mxfzef2qop`
5090: The :t:`type` of an :t:`await expression` is
5091: ``<_ as core::future::IntoFuture>::Output``.
5092: 
5093: :dp:`fls_l396mo6k9ox7`
5094: The :t:`value` of an :t:`await expression` is the :t:`value` held by
5095: :std:`core::task::Poll::Ready`.
5096: 
5097: .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
5098: 
5099: :dp:`fls_1ppywe40s62c`
5100: The :t:`evaluation` of an :t:`await expression` proceeds as follows:
5101: 
5102: #. :dp:`fls_eymcs2rgv3qw`
5103:    The :t:`future operand` is evaluated to a :t:`temporary` by invoking
5104:    :std:`core::future::IntoFuture::into_future` with the :t:`future operand`.
5105: 
5106: #. :dp:`fls_qshnnpirkasz`
5107:    The :t:`temporary` is pinned using :std:`core::pin::Pin::new_unchecked`.
5108: 
5109: #. :dp:`fls_umevprl99y6c`
5110:    The pinned :t:`temporary` is polled using :std:`core::future::Future::poll`,
5111:    passing in the :std:`core::task::Context` of the current task.
5112: 
5113: #. :dp:`fls_k76d8ady623m`
5114:    If :std:`core::future::Future::poll` returns
5115:    :std:`core::task::Poll::Pending`, then the current :t:`future` yields.
5116: 
5117: #. :dp:`fls_frwtufwe8dya`
5118:    If :std:`core::future::Future::poll` returns :std:`core::task::Poll::Ready`,
5119:    then
5120: 
5121:    #. :dp:`fls_u72ylhlmqge3`
5122:       The :t:`value` held within is unwrapped.
5123: 
5124:    #. :dp:`fls_tn3vwidct3ks`
5125:       Control stops the evaluation of the :t:`await expression`.
5126: 
5127: .. rubric:: Examples
5128: 
5129: .. code-block:: rust
5130: 
5131:    let future = async { expensive_function() };
5132:    future.await;
5133: 
5134: .. _fls_kw25194gpael:
5135: 
5136: Expression Precedence
5137: ---------------------
5138: 
5139: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
5140: 
5141: :dp:`fls_cwt7afsbgs7w`
5142: Certain :t:`[expression]s` are subject to :t:`precedence` and
5143: :dt:`associativity`.
5144: 
5145: :dp:`fls_ya23jjg5wjl`
5146: :dt:`Precedence` is the order by which :t:`[expression]s` are evaluated in the
5147: presence of other :t:`[expression]s`.
5148: 
5149: :dp:`fls_bezkcuwp5qol`
5150: :t:`Associativity` is the order by which :t:`[operand]s` are evaluated within a
5151: single :t:`expression`.
5152: 
5153: :dp:`fls_48br7odx6nke`
5154: The :t:`precedence` and :t:`associativity` of qualifying :t:`[expression]s` are
5155: as follows:
5156: 
5157: .. list-table::
5158: 
5159:    * - :dp:`fls_mk2yk99p6nvp`
5160:      - **Expression**
5161:      - **Precedence**
5162:      - **Associativity**
5163:    * - :dp:`fls_jtdnf0vmn6xt`
5164:      - :t:`Array expression`
5165: 
5166:        :t:`Block expression`
5167:

5169: 
5170:        :t:`If expression`
5171: 
5172:        :t:`If let expression`
5173:

5181:

5183:

5185: 
5186:        :t:`Tuple expression`
5187: 
5188:        :t:`Underscore expression`

5190:      - none

5197:

5200:      - left-to-right

5260:      - :t:`Lazy and expression`

5264:      - :t:`Lazy or expression`
5265:      -

5272:      - :t:`Assignment expression`
5273: 
5274:        :t:`Compound assignment expression`
5275:      -
5276:      - right-to-left
5277:    * - :dp:`fls_hr4kokysrjop`
5278:      - :t:`Break expression`
5279: 
5280:        :t:`Closure expression`
5281: 
5282:        :t:`Return expression`
5283:      - lowest
5284:      - none
5285: 
5286: .. _fls_jmjn8jkbzujm:
5287: 
5288: Capturing
5289: ---------
5290: 
5291: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
5292: 
5293: :dp:`fls_iamnzlm430ef`
5294: A :dt:`capturing expression` is either an :t:`async block expression` or a
5295: :t:`closure expression`.
5296: 
5297: :dp:`fls_eca6tl7j0afx`
5298: A :dt:`capture target` is either a :t:`variable` or a :t:`field` of a
5299: :t:`variable`.
5300: 
5301: :dp:`fls_e70ywb8191h`
5302: The :dt:`capturing environment` of a :t:`capturing expression` consists of the
5303: :t:`[value]s` of all :t:`captured` :t:`[capture target]s`.
5304: 
5305: :dp:`fls_1y2ttb466m9c`
5306: :dt:`capturing` is the process of saving the :t:`[capture target]s` of a
5307: :t:`[capturing expression]'s` :t:`capturing environment`.
5308: 
5309: :dp:`fls_ip81lt2mm940`
5310: A :t:`capture target` requires :t:`capturing` when it is used by
5311: the :t:`capturing expression` and it is defined outside of the
5312: :t:`capturing expression`. Such a :t:`capture target` is said to be
5313: :dt:`captured`.
5314: 
5315: :dp:`fls_y9n1i4hbq8sf`
5316: :dt:`capture mode` is the mechanism by which a :t:`capture target` is captured.
5317: 
5318: :dp:`fls_O6WYL8AUyPje`
5319: A :t:`captured` :t:`capture target` with :t:`capture mode` :dt:`by value capture`
5320: :t:`passes <passing convention>` the :t:`value` of the :t:`capture target` into
5321: the :t:`capturing environment`.
5322: 
5323: :dp:`fls_aCxt2Ovmb5He`
5324: A :t:`captured` :t:`capture target` with :t:`capture mode`
5325: :dt:`by immutable reference capture` binds an :dt:`immutable reference` to the
5326: :t:`capture target` and passes the :t:`immutable reference` into the
5327: :t:`capturing environment`.
5328: 
5329: :dp:`fls_xTNFfkxHm5yy`
5330: A :t:`captured` :t:`capture target` with :t:`capture mode`
5331: :dt:`by mutable reference capture` binds a :dt:`mutable reference` to the
5332: :t:`capture target` and passes the :t:`mutable reference` into the
5333: :t:`capturing environment`.
5334: 
5335: :dp:`fls_k2v7m1p9t4qa`
5336: A :dt:`unique immutable reference` is an :t:`immutable reference` produced by
5337: :t:`capturing` what is asserted to be the only live :t:`reference` to a
5338: :t:`value` while the :t:`reference` exists.
5339: 
5340: :dp:`fls_8HLaLAIZgYfs`
5341: A :t:`captured` :t:`capture target` with :t:`capture mode`
5342: :dt:`by unique immutable reference capture` binds a
5343: :t:`unique immutable reference` to the :t:`capture target` and passes the
5344: :t:`mutable reference` into the :t:`capturing environment`.
5345: 
5346: :dp:`fls_t695ps4lfh6z`
5347: The :t:`capture mode` is determined based on the use of the :t:`capture target`
5348: within the :t:`capturing expression`, as follows:
5349: 
5350: #. :dp:`fls_6j8cr7d5zs1l`
5351:    If the :t:`capturing expression` is subject to :t:`keyword` ``move``, then
5352:    the :t:`capture mode` is :t:`by value capture`.
5353: 
5354: #. :dp:`fls_l8e98pyhm08g`
5355:    Otherwise the :t:`capture mode` is determined based on the following
5356:    precedence:
5357: 
5358:    #. :dp:`fls_33hfay24hx8u`
5359:       :t:`By immutable reference capture`.
5360: 
5361:    #. :dp:`fls_wmxsd0i2yemf`
5362:       :t:`By unique immutable reference capture` mode, if the
5363:       :t:`capture target` is a :t:`mutable reference` that is being modified.
5364: 
5365:    #. :dp:`fls_lu779ufqhggl`
5366:       :t:`By mutable reference capture` mode.
5367: 
5368:    #. :dp:`fls_uqy5w9uc8gla`
5369:       :t:`By value capture`.
5370: 
5371: :dp:`fls_wvob7114tfat`
5372: A tool selects the first :t:`capture mode` that is compatible with the use of
5373: the :t:`capture target`.
5374: 
5375: .. _fls_ZfIBiJMf8qE1:
5376: 
5377: Arithmetic Overflow
5378: -------------------
5379: 
5380: :dp:`fls_oFIRXBPXu6Zv`
5381: An :dt:`arithmetic overflow` occurs when an :t:`operator expression` computes a
5382: :t:`value` of a :dt:`scalar type` that lies outside of the range of valid
5383: :t:`[value]s` for the :t:`scalar type` or when one or more :t:`operand` of an
5384: :t:`operator expression` lies outside of the range of valid :t:`[value]s` for
5385: the operation.
5386: 
5387: .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
5388: 
5389: :dp:`fls_8fU3h8PLasqA`
5390: There are two allowed behaviors for :t:`arithmetic overflow`:
5391: 
5392: #. :dp:`fls_R48VKcEIbfXC`
5393:    :t:`Evaluation` of the :t:`expression` may result in a :t:`panic`.
5394: 
5395: #. :dp:`fls_QMpI8K43K2yU`
5396:    The resulting :t:`value` of the :t:`expression` may be truncated, discarding
5397:    the most significant bits that do not fit in the target :t:`type`.
5398:

<content>1: .. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT OR Apache-2.0
2:    SPDX-FileCopyrightText: The Ferrocene Developers
3: 
4: .. default-domain:: spec
5: 
6: .. _fls_ronnwodjjjsh:
7: 
8: Ownership and Destruction
9: =========================
10: 
11: .. _fls_svkx6szhr472:
12: 
13: Ownership
14: ---------
15: 
16: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
17: 
18: :dp:`fls_wt81sbsecmu0`
19: :dt:`Ownership` is a property of :t:`[value]s` that is central to the resource
20: management model of Rust.
21: 
22: :dp:`fls_ckcnkbb6y3cq`
23: An :dt:`owner` is a :t:`variable` that holds a :t:`value`.
24: 
25: :dp:`fls_ze0u9gfylmhn`
26: A :t:`value` shall have only one :t:`owner`.
27: 
28: .. _fls_3xvm61x0t251:
29: 
30: Initialization
31: --------------
32: 
33: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
34: 
35: :dp:`fls_drfzu02bo7oe`
36: :dt:`Initialization` is the act of supplying an initial :t:`value` to a
37: :t:`variable`.
38: 
39: :dp:`fls_hl5tnd9yy252`
40: A :t:`variable` is a placeholder for a :t:`value` that is allocated on the
41: stack.
42: 
43: :dp:`fls_wnhci8phdu4m`
44: When a :t:`variable` holds a :t:`value`, the :t:`variable` is considered to be
45: :dt:`initialized`.
46: 
47: :dp:`fls_ch2lvm50olqd`
48: When a :t:`variable` lacks a :t:`value` or its :t:`value` has been
49: :t:`passed <passing convention>` :t:`by move`, the :t:`variable` is considered
50: to be :dt:`uninitialized`.
51: 
52: :dp:`fls_46910buiwvv9`
53: A :t:`variable` shall be :t:`initialized` before it is accessed.
54: 
55: .. rubric:: Runtime Semantics
56: 
57: :dp:`fls_caufcwkpz689`
58: All memory starts as uninitialized.
59: 
60: .. rubric:: Examples
61: 
62: :dp:`fls_4lg92b9yima6`
63: Variable ``a`` is initialized.
64: 
65: .. code-block:: rust
66: 
67:    let a: i32 = 42;
68: 
69: :dp:`fls_xn1au0blioa3`
70: Variable ``b`` starts off as uninitialized, but is later initialized by virtue
71: of the assignment statement.
72: 
73: .. code-block:: rust
74: 
75:    let b: i32;
76:    b = 42;
77: 
78: :dp:`fls_jmcjboopvytb`
79: Variable ``c`` starts off initialized, but is later uninitialized by virtue of a
80: transfer :t:`by move`.
81: 
82: .. code-block:: rust
83: 
84:    use core::sync::atomic::AtomicI32;
85: 
86:    let c: AtomicI32 = AtomicI32::new(42);
87:    let d: AtomicI32 = c;
88: 
89: .. _fls_v5x85lt5ulva:
90: 
91: References
92: ----------
93: 
94: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
95: 
96: :dp:`fls_7x9pi2o7pee7`
97: A :t:`reference` is a :t:`value` of a :t:`reference type`. A :t:`reference`
98: can be obtained explicitly by using a :t:`borrow expression` or implicitly in
99: certain scenarios.
100: 
101: :dp:`fls_2s9h4k8v1qpm`
102: A :dt:`reference` is a :t:`value` of a :t:`reference type`.
103: 
104: :dp:`fls_tsqvr3fmcel`
105: A :dt:`referent` is the :t:`value` pointed-to by a :t:`reference`.
106: 
107: :dp:`fls_ev4a82fdhwr8`
108: A :t:`reference` shall point to an :t:`initialized` :t:`referent`.
109: 
110: :dp:`fls_cckf6dtkgwb4`
111: The :t:`lifetime` of a :t:`referent` shall be at least as long as the
112: :t:`lifetime` of its :t:`reference`.
113: 
114: :dp:`fls_8kqb8754e6p4`
115: A :t:`reference` is :dt:`active` from the point of obtaining its :t:`referent`
116: upto the last use of the :t:`reference`, prior to another assignment to the
117: :t:`reference` or the end of the :t:`scope` of the :t:`reference`.
118: 
119: :dp:`fls_v69rptdjao42`
120: A :t:`referent` shall not be :t:`passed <passing convention>` :t:`by move` while
121: a :t:`reference` to it is :t:`active`.
122: 
123: :dp:`fls_vg9h6tz6z37w`
124: A :t:`referent` shall not be modified while a :t:`reference` to it is
125: :t:`active`.
126: 
127: :dp:`fls_wcf5mxrzbujn`
128: An :t:`immutable reference` is a :t:`value` of a :t:`shared reference type`, and
129: prevents the mutation of its :t:`referent`.
130: 
131: :dp:`fls_4m2v8k1q9p0s`
132: A :t:`shared reference` is a :t:`value` of a :t:`shared reference type`.
133: 
134: :dp:`fls_fckoj1jh5mrc`
135: A :t:`mutable reference` is a :t:`value` of a :t:`mutable reference type`, and
136: allows the mutation of its :t:`referent`.
137: 
138: :dp:`fls_hqxsuyn285he`
139: The :t:`referent` of an :t:`immutable reference` shall be mutated only when the
140: :t:`type` of the :t:`referent` is subject to :t:`interior mutability`.
141: 
142: :dp:`fls_i1ny0k726a4a`
143: While a :t:`mutable reference` is :t:`active`, no other :t:`reference` shall
144: refer to a :t:`value` that :t:`[overlap]s` with the :t:`referent` of the
145: :t:`mutable reference`.
146: 
147: .. rubric:: Undefined Behavior
148: 
149: :dp:`fls_eT1hnLOx6vxk`
150: It is undefined behavior to access a :t:`value` through aliasing
151: :t:`[mutable reference]s` from :t:`unsafe context`.
152: 
153: .. rubric:: Examples
154: 
155: .. code-block:: rust
156: 
157:    let immutable_reference: &i32 = &42;
158:    let mutable_reference: &mut i32 = &mut 42;
159: 
160: .. _fls_a14slch83hzn:
161: 
162: Borrowing
163: ---------
164: 
165: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
166: 
167: :dp:`fls_c02flohk54pc`
168: :dt:`Borrowing` is the process of temporarily associating a :t:`reference` with a
169: :t:`value` without transferring :t:`ownership` permanently.
170: 
171: :dp:`fls_j9kof0px3l7s`
172: A :t:`borrow` is a :t:`reference` produced by :t:`borrowing`.
173: 
174: :dp:`fls_yL6cMahJUIqO`
175: A :t:`value` is :t:`borrowed` when it is associated with an active
176: :t:`borrow`.
177: 
178: :dp:`fls_wb031borrowed`
179: :dt:`borrowed` is the state of a memory location when a :t:`reference`
180: pointing to it is :t:`active`.
181: 
182: :dp:`fls_zepwytjwy049`
183: An :t:`implicit borrow` is a :t:`borrow` that is not present syntactically in
184: program text. An :t:`implicit borrow` occurs in the following contexts:
185: 
186: * :dp:`fls_nordokzfy36d`
187:   The :t:`call operand` of a :t:`call expression`,
188: 
189: * :dp:`fls_bjf3futso849`
190:   The :t:`[operand]s` of a :t:`comparison expression`,
191: 
192: * :dp:`fls_yfmy4v5zlgw9`
193:   The :t:`assigned operand` of a :t:`compound assignment expression`,
194: 
195: * :dp:`fls_jv18y618j2s3`
196:   The :t:`operand` of a :t:`field access expression`,
197: 
198: * :dp:`fls_g4i0jb27iryr`
199:   The :t:`operand` of a :t:`dereference expression`,
200: 
201: * :dp:`fls_kky9ufexrvaw`
202:   The :t:`indexed operand` of an :t:`index expression`,
203: 
204: * :dp:`fls_o5oq4jfswr4q`
205:   The :t:`receiver operand` of a :t:`method call expression`.
206: 
207: :dp:`fls_MT69AGRXH9pS``
208: An :t:`implicit borrow` may be an :dt:`immutable borrow` or a :t:`mutable borrow`
209: if required.
210: 
211: :dp:`fls_hyl4bdjbuzbw`
212: An :t:`immutable borrow` is an :t:`immutable reference` produced by
213: :t:`borrowing`.
214: 
215: :dp:`fls_8q0n7v3k2m1b`
216: A :dt:`shared borrow` is a :t:`borrow` produced by evaluating an
217: :t:`immutable borrow expression`.
218: 
219: :dp:`fls_pu19i4sj6yg0`
220: A :dt:`mutable borrow` is a :t:`mutable reference` produced by :t:`borrowing`.
221: 
222: :dp:`fls_kxws4zmaahj6`
223: :t:`Borrowing` a :t:`field` of a :t:`union type` borrows all remaining
224: :t:`[field]s` using the same :t:`lifetime`.
225: 
226: :dp:`fls_kup2ou22nwyl`
227: Immutably :t:`borrowing` a :t:`value` proceeds as follows:
228: 
229: #. :dp:`fls_8q5ly4x104ai`
230:    An :t:`immutable borrow` of :t:`type` ``&'a T`` is created, where
231:    :t:`lifetime` ``'a`` is replaced by a :t:`lifetime variable`, and
232:    ``T`` is replaced by the borrowed :t:`type`.
233: 
234: #. :dp:`fls_yhchu2bpil4m`
235:    Lifetime inference is performed.
236: 
237: #. :dp:`fls_568o7nyihndd`
238:    The :t:`immutable borrow` is checked against other :t:`[borrow]s` and
239:    :t:`by move` passing within the enclosing :t:`item`.
240: 
241: #. :dp:`fls_Z2n7EJiLY5CT`
242:    An :t:`immutable reference` to the borrowed :t:`value` is produced.
243: 
244: #. :dp:`fls_ibi9mqGeHNLp`
245:    The :t:`immutable borrow` is released immediately after the last usage of its
246:    related :t:`immutable reference`.
247: 
248: :dp:`fls_f9we73i8vwq3`
249: Mutably :t:`borrowing` a :t:`value` proceeds as follows:
250: 
251: #. :dp:`fls_w5bjgaov8w60`
252:    A :t:`mutable borrow` of :t:`type` ``&'a mut T`` is created, where

254:    ``T`` is replaced by the borrowed :t:`type`.
255: 
256: #. :dp:`fls_gbqizu6gu6kk`
257:    Lifetime inference is performed.

140: .. _fls_7bxv8lybxm18:
141: 
142: Identifier Patterns
143: -------------------
144: 
145: .. rubric:: Syntax
146: 
147: .. syntax::
148: 
149:    IdentifierPattern ::=
150:        $$ref$$? $$mut$$? Binding BoundPattern?
151: 
152:    BoundPattern ::=
153:        $$@$$ Pattern
154: 
155: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
156: 
157: :dp:`fls_uljdw9rf7ies`
158: An :dt:`identifier pattern` is a :t:`pattern` that binds the :t:`value` it
159: matches to a :t:`binding`.
160: 
161: :dp:`fls_vy9uw586wy0d`
162: A :dt:`bound pattern` is a :t:`pattern` that imposes a constraint on a related
163: :t:`identifier pattern`.
164: 
165: :dp:`fls_hqwt3fvr063y`
166: An :t:`identifier pattern` yields a :t:`binding`. An :t:`identifier pattern`
167: with :t:`keyword` ``mut`` yields a :t:`mutable binding`.
168: 
169: :dp:`fls_joIQdDn44oIT`
170: A :t:`reference identifier pattern` is an :t:`identifier pattern` with
171: :t:`keyword` ``ref``.
172: 
173: :dp:`fls_r2m9k6t1v4pa`
174: A :dt:`unit enum variant` is an :t:`enum variant` without a :t:`field list`.
175: 
176: :dp:`fls_6c1v8m4p2tqa`
177: A :dt:`unit struct constant` is a :t:`constant` implicitly created by a
178: :t:`unit struct`.
179: 
180: :dp:`fls_24c95c56tugl`
181: The :t:`identifier pattern` enters its :t:`binding` into :t:`binding scope` in
182: the :t:`value namespace` if it does not resolve to a :t:`constant`, a
183: :t:`unit struct constant` or a :t:`unit enum variant`.
184: 
185: :dp:`fls_twcavjk7iquy`
186: It is a static error if the :t:`identifier pattern` consists of anything other
187: than a :t:`binding` when the :t:`binding` resolves to a :t:`constant`, a
188: :t:`unit struct constant`, or a :t:`unit enum variant`.
189: 
190: :dp:`fls_k1yBTstX7jEE`
191: It is a static error if the :t:`binding` of an :t:`identifier pattern` resolves
192: to a :t:`tuple struct` or a :t:`tuple enum variant`.
193: 
194: :dp:`fls_hw26hy33guk5`
195: An :t:`identifier pattern` is an :t:`irrefutable pattern` when:
196: 
197: * :dp:`fls_svfxwz4yy5i`
198:   It has a :t:`bound pattern` and the :t:`bound pattern` is an
199:   :t:`irrefutable pattern`, or
200: 
201: * :dp:`fls_x6f6q22b5jpc`
202:   It does not have a :t:`bound pattern` and its :t:`binding` resolves to an
203:   :t:`irrefutable constant`.
204: 
205: * :dp:`fls_r2mb8v2lh3x0`
206:   It does not have a :t:`bound pattern` and its :t:`binding` does not resolve to
207:   a :t:`constant`, a :t:`unit struct constant`, or a :t:`unit enum variant`.
208: 
209: :dp:`fls_7oioaitb075g`
210: If the :t:`identifier pattern` does not have a :t:`bound pattern`, then the
211: :t:`type` of its :t:`binding` is determined as follows:
212: 
213: * :dp:`fls_40qin0ss5sqd`
214:   If the :t:`identifier pattern` has only :t:`keyword` ``ref``, then the
215:   :t:`type` is ``& inferred_type``, where ``inferred_type`` is the :t:`type`
216:   determined by :t:`type inference`.
217: 
218: * :dp:`fls_pivz0v7ey6sw`
219:   If the :t:`identifier pattern` has :t:`[keyword]s` ``ref`` ``mut``, then the
220:   :t:`type` is ``&mut inferred_type``, where ``inferred_type`` is the :t:`type`
221:   determined by :t:`type inference`.
222: 
223: * :dp:`fls_2ahkrddxwj1n`
224:   Otherwise the :t:`type` is ``inferred_type``, where ``inferred_type`` is the
225:   :t:`type` determined by :t:`type inference`.
226: 
227: :dp:`fls_eucnafj3uedy`
228: If the :t:`identifier pattern` has a :t:`bound pattern`, then the :t:`type` of
229: its :t:`binding` is determined as follows:
230: 
231: * :dp:`fls_f8zo4scodhcr`
232:   If the :t:`identifier pattern` has only :t:`keyword` ``ref``, then the
233:   :t:`type` is ``& bound_pattern_type``, where ``bound_pattern_type`` is the
234:   :t:`type` of the :t:`bound pattern`.
235: 
236: * :dp:`fls_d3fs2h7oqjl0`
237:   If the :t:`identifier pattern` has :t:`[keyword]s` ``ref mut``, then the
238:   :t:`type` is ``&mut bound_pattern_type``, where ``bound_pattern_type`` is the
239:   :t:`type` of the :t:`bound pattern`.
240: 
241: * :dp:`fls_exo8asevh5x1`
242:   Otherwise the :t:`type` is ``inferred_type``, where ``inferred_type`` is the
243:   :t:`type` determined by :t:`type inference`.
244: 
245: .. rubric:: Examples
246:

521: A :dt:`range pattern low bound` is a :t:`range pattern bound` that specifies the

525: A :dt:`range pattern high bound` is a :t:`range pattern bound` that specifies the

527: 
528: :dp:`fls_2hpuccwh2xml`
529: A :t:`half-open range pattern` shall appear within a :t:`parenthesized pattern`
530: when the context is a :t:`slice pattern`.
531: 
532: :dp:`fls_9kk81isk0mlp`
533: The :t:`range pattern low bound` of an :t:`inclusive range pattern` shall be
534: less than or equal to its :t:`range pattern high bound`.
535: 
536: :dp:`fls_8bdOqkO1NuJW`
537: The :t:`range pattern low bound` of an :t:`exclusive range pattern` shall be
538: less than its :t:`range pattern high bound`.
539: 
540: :dp:`fls_s2b5n4snc4d7`
541: An :t:`obsolete range pattern` is equivalent to an :t:`inclusive range pattern`.
542: 
543: :dp:`fls_4o4ge6x9a8rs`

545: entire set of possible :t:`[value]s` of a :t:`type`.

548: The :t:`[type]s` of the :t:`range pattern low bound` and the
549: :t:`range pattern high bound` of a :t:`range pattern` shall be :t:`unifiable`.
550:

564: A :t:`path expression` of a :t:`range pattern` shall refer to a :t:`constant`
565: of a :t:`scalar type`.

32: 
33: * :dp:`fls_4akfvpq1yg4g`
34:   :t:`Attribute` :c:`no_mangle`.
35: 
36: * :dp:`fls_9d8v0xeyi0f`
37:   :t:`Attribute` :c:`used`.
38: 
39: .. _fls_usgd0xlijoxv:
40: 
41: ABI
42: ---
43: 
44: .. rubric:: Syntax
45: 
46: .. syntax::
47: 
48:    AbiSpecification ::=
49:        $$extern$$ AbiKind?
50: 
51:    AbiKind ::=
52:        RawStringLiteral
53:      | StringLiteral
54: 
55: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
56: 
57: :dp:`fls_xangrq3tfze0`
58: An :dt:`Application Binary Interface` is a set of conventions that dictate how
59: data and computation cross language boundaries.
60: 
61: :dp:`fls_q2wp45ZVk16O`
62: An :dt:`ABI` is an :t:`Application Binary Interface`.
63: 
64: :dp:`fls_EcG0AVDg8TUj`
65: In this specification, :t:`C` refers to the C programming language used for
66: foreign function interfaces.
67: 
68: :dp:`fls_2w0xi6rxw3uz`
69: The :dt:`ABI kind` indicates the :t:`ABI` of a :t:`construct`.
70: 
71: :dp:`fls_9zitf1fvvfk8`
72: The following :t:`[ABI]s` are supported:
73: 
74: * :dp:`fls_x7ct9k82fpgn`
75:   ``extern "C"`` - The default :t:`ABI` of :t:`C` code, referred to as
76:   :dt:`extern C ABI`.
77: 
78: * :dp:`fls_LfjvLXvI6TFL`
79:   ``extern "C-unwind"`` - The same as ``extern "C"`` with the addition that
80:   unwinding across FFI is permitted.
81: 
82: * :dp:`fls_a2d8ltpgtvn6`
83:   ``extern "Rust"`` - The default :t:`ABI` of a Rust program, referred to as
84:   :dt:`Rust ABI`.
85: 
86: * :dp:`fls_8m7pc3riokst`
87:   ``extern "system"`` - The operating system-dependent :t:`ABI`. This :t:`ABI kind` is equivalent to ``extern "C"`` except on Windows x86_32 where it is equivalent to ``extern "stdcall"`` for non-:t:`[variadic function]s`, and equivalent to ``extern "C"`` for :t:`[variadic function]s`.
88: 
89: * :dp:`fls_NQAzj5ai1La5`
90:   ``extern "system-unwind"`` - The same as ``extern "system"`` with the
91:   addition that unwinding across FFI is permitted.
92: 
93: :dp:`fls_r2drzo3dixe4`
94: A :t:`function` without an explicit :t:`ABI` has implicit :t:`Rust ABI`, unless
95: it appears within an :t:`external block`.
96: 
97: :dp:`fls_z2kzyin8dyr7`
98: A :t:`function` with an :t:`ABI` but without a specified :t:`ABI kind` has
99: implicit :t:`extern C ABI`.
100: 
101: .. rubric:: Implementation Permissions
102: 
103: :dp:`fls_j6pqchx27ast`
104: A tool is allowed to specify additional :t:`[ABI]s`. These :t:`[ABI]s` may
105: include, but may not be limited to, the following:
106: 
107: * :dp:`fls_dbbfqaqa80r8`
108:   ``extern "aapcs"`` - The soft-float ABI for 32-bit ARM targets.
109: 
110: * :dp:`fls_UippZpUyYpHl`
111:   ``extern "aapcs-unwind"`` - The same as ``extern "aapcs"``
112:   with the addition that unwinding across FFI is permitted.
113: 
114: * :dp:`fls_36qrs2fxxvi7`
115:   ``extern "cdecl"`` - The x86_32 :t:`ABI` of :t:`C` code.
116: 
117: * :dp:`fls_CIyK8BYzzo26`
118:   ``extern "cdecl-unwind"`` - The same as ``extern "cdecl"``
119:   with the addition that unwinding across FFI is permitted.
120: 
121: * :dp:`fls_JHlqXjn4Sf07`
122:   ``extern "efiapi"`` - The :t:`ABI` for `UEFI <https://uefi.org/specifications>`_.
123: 
124: * :dp:`fls_6rtj6rwqxojh`
125:   ``extern "fastcall"`` - The ``fastcall`` :t:`ABI` that corresponds to MSVC's
126:   ``__fastcall`` and GCC and clang's ``__attribute__((fastcall))``.
127: 
128: * :dp:`fls_d3nmpc5mtg27`
129:   ``extern "stdcall"`` - The x86_32 :t:`ABI` of the Win32 API.
130: 
131: * :dp:`fls_7t7yxh94wnbl`
132:   ``extern "sysv64"`` - The x86_64 non-Windows :t:`ABI` of :t:`C` code.
133: 
134: * :dp:`fls_ccFdnlX5HIYk`
135:   ``extern "sysv64-unwind"`` - The same as ``extern "sysv64"``
136:   with the addition that unwinding across FFI is permitted.
137: 
138: * :dp:`fls_sxj4vy39sj4g`
139:   ``extern "vectorcall"`` - The ``vectorcall`` :t:`ABI` that corresponds to
140:   MSVC's ``__vectorcall`` and clang's ``__attribute__((vectorcall))``.
141: 
142: * :dp:`fls_tyjs1x4j8ovp`
143:   ``extern "win64"`` - The x86_64 Windows :t:`ABI` of :t:`C` code.
144: 
145: * :dp:`fls_xrCRprWS13R1`
146:   ``extern "win64-unwind"`` - The same as ``extern "win64"``
147:   with the addition that unwinding across FFI is permitted.
148: 
149: .. rubric:: Undefined Behavior
150: 
151: :dp:`fls_M4LqHf8hbPA8`
152: It is undefined behavior when a foreign exception crosses a
153: :t:`foreign function interface` boundary with an :t:`ABI` that does not end in
154: ``-unwind``.
155: 
156: .. rubric:: Examples
157: 
158: .. code-block:: rust
159: 
160:    extern
161:    extern "C"
162: 
163: .. _fls_tmoh3y9oyqsy:
164: 
165: External Blocks
166: ---------------
167: 
168: .. rubric:: Syntax
169: 
170: .. syntax::
171: 
172:    ExternalBlock ::=
173:        $$unsafe$$? $$extern$$ AbiKind? $${$$
174:          InnerAttributeOrDoc*
175:          ExternItem*
176:        $$}$$
177: 
178:    ExternItem ::=
179:        OuterAttributeOrDoc* (ExternalItemWithVisibility | TerminatedMacroInvocation)
180: 
181:    ExternalItemWithVisibility ::=
182:        VisibilityModifier? (
183:            FunctionDeclaration
184:          | StaticDeclaration
185:        )
186: 
187: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
188: 
189: :dp:`fls_4dje9t5y2dia`
190: An :dt:`external block` is a :t:`construct` that provides the declarations of
191: :t:`[external function]s` and :t:`[external static]s` as unchecked imports.
192: 
193: :dp:`fls_Nz0l16hMxqTd`
194: The :t:`ABI` of an :t:`external block` is determined as follows:
195: 
196: * :dp:`fls_4XOoiFloXM7t`
197:   If the :t:`external block` specifies an :s:`AbiKind`, then the :t:`ABI` is the specified :s:`AbiKind`.
198: 
199: * :dp:`fls_PBsepNHImJKH`
200:   Otherwise the :t:`ABI` is the :t:`extern C ABI`.
201: 
202: .. rubric:: Examples
203: 
204: .. code-block:: rust
205: 
206:    extern "C" {
207:        static MAX_LENGTH: size_t;
208: 
209:        fn compress
210:            (input: *const u8,
211:             input_length: size_t,
212:             compressed: *mut u8,
213:             compressed_length: *mut size_t) -> c_int;
214:        fn log(msg: *const c_char, ...);
215:    }
216: 
217: .. _fls_yztwtek0y34v:
218: 
219: External Functions
220: ------------------

FFI
===

.. rubric:: Legality Rules

:dp:`fls_djlglv2eaihl`
A :dt:`Foreign Function Interface` employs :t:`ABI`, :t:`[attribute]s`,
:t:`[external block]s`, :t:`[external function]s`, linkage, and :t:`type`
:t:`layout` to interface a Rust program with foreign code.

:dp:`fls_JpmkeFXLhQyy`
:dt:`FFI` is an abbreviation for :t:`Foreign Function Interface`.

<content>1040: .. _fls_vlrto778v49m:
1041: 
1042: Tuple Struct Patterns
1043: ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
1044: 
1045: .. rubric:: Syntax
1046: 
1047: .. syntax::
1048: 
1049:    TupleStructPattern ::=
1050:        Deconstructee $$($$ PatternList? $$)$$
1051: 
1052: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
1053: 
1054: :dp:`fls_ks6y1syab2bp`
1055: A :dt:`tuple struct pattern` is a :t:`pattern` that matches a
1056: :t:`tuple enum variant value` or a :t:`tuple struct value`.
1057: 
1058: :dp:`fls_t1mrijw16k9a`
1059: The :t:`deconstructee` of a :t:`tuple struct pattern` shall resolve to a
1060: :t:`tuple enum variant` or a :t:`tuple struct type`.
1061: 
1062: :dp:`fls_ryfcrqrkp28y`
1063: A :dt:`subpattern` of a :t:`tuple struct pattern` matches a :t:`field` of the
1064: :t:`deconstructee` when its position and the position of the :t:`field` in
1065: the :t:`deconstructee` are the same. Such a :t:`subpattern` is a
1066: :dt:`matched tuple struct subpattern`.
1067: 
1068: :dp:`fls_ehf9r6halgh1`
1069: The position of a :t:`subpattern` is determined as follows:
1070: 
1071: * :dp:`fls_5lo1hs8wzz0t`
1072:   If the :t:`tuple struct pattern` has a :s:`RecordStructRestPattern`, then
1073: 
1074:   * :dp:`fls_gwuc2xffosu`
1075:     If the :t:`subpattern` precedes the :s:`RecordStructRestPattern`, then its
1076:     position is the position within the :s:`PatternList` in left-to-right order,
1077:     starting from zero.
1078: 
1079:   * :dp:`fls_w369n8lmwr7g`
1080:     If the :t:`subpattern` succeeds the :s:`RecordStructRestPattern`, then its
1081:     position is the position within the :s:`PatternList` list in right-to-left
1082:     order, starting from the :dt:`arity` of the :t:`deconstructee` minus one.
1083: 
1084: * :dp:`fls_4is6h95jj3gd`
1085:   Otherwise the position is the position within the :s:`PatternList` in
1086:   left-to-right order, starting from zero.
1087: 
1088: :dp:`fls_budf0rpsa4lx`
1089: The :t:`type` of the :t:`subpattern` of a :t:`tuple struct pattern` and the
1090: :t:`type` of the matched :t:`field` shall be :t:`unifiable`.
1091: 
1092: :dp:`fls_vo6mtauh4qhb`
1093: For each :t:`field` of the :t:`deconstructee`, the :t:`tuple struct pattern`
1094: shall either:
1095: 
1096: * :dp:`fls_rco3fwlx2a76`
1097:   Contain at most one :t:`matched tuple struct subpattern`, or
1098: 
1099: * :dp:`fls_4vrnxslad09e`
1100:   Have exactly one :s:`RecordStructRestPattern`.
1101: 
1102: :dp:`fls_qgilaqy5zx7q`
1103: A :s:`RecordStructRestPattern` is allowed even if all :t:`[field]s` of the
1104: :t:`deconstructee` have been matched.
1105: 
1106: .. rubric:: Examples
1107: 
1108: :dp:`fls_2u99arsbnlnk`
1109: See :p:`fls_D5tAGzrjXFTu` for the declarations of ``TupleStruct`` and
1110: ``tuple_struct_value``.
1111: 
1112: .. code-block:: rust
1113: 
1114:    match tuple_struct_value {
1115:        TupleStruct ( 11, 22 ) => (),
1116:        TupleStruct ( 33, .., 44 ) => (),
1117:        TupleStruct ( .., 55 ) => (),
1118:        TupleStruct ( 66, .. ) => (),
1119:        TupleStruct ( .. ) => (),
1120:    }
1121: 
1122: .. _fls_urbr5rg9206v:
1123: 
1124: Tuple Patterns
1125: ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
1126: 
1127: .. rubric:: Syntax
1128: 
1129: .. syntax::
1130: 
1131:    TuplePattern ::=
1132:        $$($$ PatternList? $$)$$
1133: 
1134: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
1135: 
1136: :dp:`fls_e2manugp4e0b`
1137: A :dt:`tuple pattern` is a :t:`pattern` that matches a :t:`tuple` which satisfies
1138: all criteria defined by its :t:`[subpattern]s`.
1139: 
1140: :dp:`fls_xk8udu4k61kj`
1141: A :t:`tuple pattern` is an :t:`irrefutable pattern` when all of its
1142: :t:`[subpattern]s` are :t:`[irrefutable pattern]s`.
1143: 
1144: :dp:`fls_yhcaz6v49ub2`
1145: The :t:`type` of a :t:`tuple pattern` is the :t:`type` of the :t:`tuple` being
1146: destructured.
1147: 
1148: :dp:`fls_v7c3y1q9m2bx`
1149: A :dt:`tuple field` is a :t:`field` of a :t:`tuple type`.
1150: 
1151: :dp:`fls_6WCm0Ra8NQl4`
1152: A :t:`subpattern` of a :t:`tuple pattern` matches a :t:`tuple field` of the
1153: :t:`tuple type` when its position and the position of the :t:`tuple field` in
1154: the :t:`tuple type` are the same. Such a :t:`subpattern` is a
1155: :dt:`matched tuple subpattern`.
1156: 
1157: :dp:`fls_a3qvQjyilORx`
1158: The position of a :t:`subpattern` is determined as follows:
1159: 
1160: * :dp:`fls_KmIHFxlBYelZ`
1161:   If the :t:`tuple pattern` has a :s:`RestPattern`, then
1162: 
1163:   * :dp:`fls_5bXqIaKiFcLg`
1164:     If the :t:`subpattern` precedes the :s:`RestPattern`, then its
1165:     position is the position within the :s:`PatternList` in left-to-right order,
1166:     starting from zero.
1167: 
1168:   * :dp:`fls_soHCAVfGlv5f`
1169:     If the :t:`subpattern` succeeds the :s:`RestPattern`, then its
1170:     position is the position within the :s:`PatternList` list in right-to-left
1171:     order, starting from the :t:`arity` of the :t:`tuple type` minus one.
1172: 
1173: * :dp:`fls_iiKvYs61959S`
1174:   Otherwise the position is the position within the :s:`PatternList` in
1175:   left-to-right order, starting from zero.
1176: 
1177: :dp:`fls_F4k6ljuP8Amf`
1178: The :t:`type` of the :t:`subpattern` of a :t:`tuple pattern` and the
1179: :t:`type` of the matched :t:`tuple field` shall be :t:`unifiable`.
1180: 
1181: :dp:`fls_GjjCDkVJPQS8`
1182: For each :t:`tuple field` of the :t:`tuple type`, the :t:`tuple pattern` shall
1183: either:
1184: 
1185: * :dp:`fls_9Qw9N87swwNe`
1186:   Contain at most one :t:`matched tuple subpattern`, or
1187: 
1188: * :dp:`fls_CQ84wkLyrAJv`
1189:   Have exactly one :s:`RestPattern`.
1190: 
1191: :dp:`fls_cC6ohNuiltfL`
1192: A :s:`RestPattern` is allowed even if all :t:`[tuple field]s` of the
1193: :t:`tuple type` have been matched.
1194: 
1195: .. rubric:: Examples
1196: 
1197: :dp:`fls_8r81vtv5hnrd`
1198: A tuple pattern in the context of a let statement.
1199: 
1200: .. code-block:: rust
1201: 
1202:    let pair = (1, "two");
1203:    let (first, second) = pair;
1204:

219: External Functions
220: ------------------
221: 
222: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
223: 
224: :dp:`fls_v24ino4hix3m`
225: An :t:`external function` is an unchecked import of a foreign :t:`function`.
226: 
227: :dp:`fls_8ltVLtAfvy0m`
228: An :dt:`unsafe external block` is an :t:`external block` subject to keyword
229: ``unsafe``.
230: 
231: :dp:`fls_l88r9fj82650`
232: An :t:`external function` shall be invoked from an :t:`unsafe context` unless it is defined in an :t:`unsafe external block` and subject to :s:`ItemSafety` with keyword ``safe``.
233: 
234: :dp:`fls_qwchgvvnp0qe`
235: An :t:`external function` shall not specify a :s:`FunctionQualifierList`.
236: 
237: :dp:`fls_w00qi1gx204e`
238: An :t:`external function` inherits the :t:`ABI` of its enclosing
239: :t:`external block`.
240: 
241: :dp:`fls_m7tu4w4lk8v`
242: An :t:`external function` shall not specify a :s:`GenericParameterList`
243: containing :t:`[constant parameter]s` or :t:`[type parameter]s`.
244: 
245: :dp:`fls_rdu4723vp0oo`
246: An :t:`external function` shall not specify a :s:`FunctionBody`.
247: 
248: :dp:`fls_9div9yusw64h`
249: An :t:`external function` shall not specify :t:`[pattern]s` other than
250: :t:`[identifier pattern]s` and :t:`[underscore pattern]s`.
251: 
252: :dp:`fls_juob30rst11r`
253: Only the last parameter :s:`FunctionParameter` of an :t:`external function` may
254: specify a :s:`FunctionParameterVariadicPart`.
255: 
256: .. _fls_s4yt19sptl7d:
257: 
258: External Statics
259: ----------------
260: 
261: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
262: 
263: :dp:`fls_8ddsytjr4il6`
264: An :t:`external static` is an import of a foreign :t:`variable`.
265: 
266: :dp:`fls_wb026immutable`
267: An :dt:`immutable` :t:`value` is a :t:`value` that cannot be modified.
268: 
269: :dp:`fls_H0cg9XMaGz0y`
270: An :t:`external static` inherits the :t:`ABI` of its enclosing
271: :t:`external block`.
272: 
273: :dp:`fls_fo9with6xumo`
274: An :t:`external static` shall be referenced from an :t:`unsafe context` unless it is defined in an :t:`unsafe external block` and subject to :s:`ItemSafety` with keyword ``safe``.
275: 
276: :dp:`fls_tr7purzcldn0`
277: An :t:`external static` shall not specify a :t:`static initializer`.

281: :dp:`fls_en2h09ehj0j3`
282: An :t:`immutable` :t:`external static` shall be initialized before Rust code

3098: Object Safety
3099: ~~~~~~~~~~~~~

3101: .. rubric:: Legality Rules

3103: :dp:`fls_aG0l4OGm8sMG`
3104: :dt:`Object safety` is the process of determining whether a :t:`trait` can be used
3105: as a :t:`trait object type`.
3106: 
3107: :dp:`fls_lrdki56hpc3k`
3108: A :t:`trait` is :dt:`object safe` when:
3109: 
3110: * :dp:`fls_5wlltclogfkw`
3111:   Its :t:`[supertrait]s` are :t:`object safe`, and
3112: 
3113: * :dp:`fls_droy0w5gtqaw`
3114:   :std:`core::marker::Sized` is not a :t:`supertrait`, and
3115: 
3116: * :dp:`fls_46gd1q80c6bn`
3117:   It lacks :t:`[associated constant]s`, and
3118: 
3119: * :dp:`fls_kwo4cknx0yat`
3120:   Its :t:`[associated function]s` are :t:`object safe`, and
3121: 
3122: * :dp:`fls_vmLLL82EQasI`
3123:   Its :t:`[associated type]s` specify a :std:`core::marker::Sized`
3124:   :t:`[trait bound]` for :c:`Self` in a :dt:`type bound predicate`.
3125: 
3126: :dp:`fls_lj7492aq7fzo`
3127: An :t:`associated function` is a :t:`function` that appears as an
3128: :t:`associated item`.
3129: 
3130: :dp:`fls_uixekv82g2e5`
3131: An :t:`associated function` is :t:`object safe` when it is either an
3132: :t:`object safe` dispatchable :t:`function` or an :t:`object safe`
3133: non-dispatchable :t:`function`.
3134: 
3135: :dp:`fls_72tvfoemwpyy`
3136: A dispatchable :t:`function` is :t:`object safe` when:
3137: 
3138: * :dp:`fls_j7nb34o87l1z`
3139:   It lacks :t:`[generic parameter]s`, and
3140: 
3141: * :dp:`fls_k1vc9vd8at92`

3143:   except in the :t:`type` of its :dt:`self parameter` or as the :t:`type` of a

1661:     The leftmost :t:`path segment` resolves to the :t:`qualified type`, and
1662: 
1663:   * :dp:`fls_xujlscsir05f`
1664:     If the :t:`qualified type` is subject to a :t:`qualifying trait`, then the
1665:     :t:`resolution context` of the leftmost :t:`path segment` consists of the
1666:     :t:`entities <entity>` of all :t:`[implementation]s` of the
1667:     :t:`qualified type` that implement the :t:`qualifying trait`.
1668: 
1669:   * :dp:`fls_wypnvfklnmc1`
1670:     Otherwise the :t:`resolution context` of the leftmost :t:`path segment`
1671:     consists of the :t:`entity` of the :t:`qualified type`, the
1672:     :t:`entities <entity>` of all its :t:`[inherent implementation]s`, and the
1673:     :t:`entities <entity>` of all its :t:`[trait implementation]s` of
1674:     :t:`[trait]s` that are :t:`in scope`.
1675: 
1676: :dp:`fls_zi46lmwsn4rg`
1677: The resolution of the rightmost :t:`path segment` is determined based on the
1678: :t:`path resolution` kind, where the :t:`name` of the
1679: :t:`candidate selected entity` is restricted by the :t:`namespace context`.
1680: 
1681: :dp:`fls_bATFGtxjKq0B`
1682: It is a static error if the leftmost :t:`path segment` is an :t:`identifier`
1683: introduced by the :t:`external prelude` that is also :t:`shadowed <shadowing>`.
1684: 
1685: .. _fls_bbso3c45kr9z:
1686: 
1687: Simple Path Resolution
1688: ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
1689: 
1690: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
1691: 
1692: :dp:`fls_uml24jw5jo7a`
1693: :dt:`simple path resolution` is a kind of :t:`path resolution` that applies to
1694: a :t:`simple path`.
1695: 
1696: :dp:`fls_59wd7loxst43`
1697: The :t:`namespace context` of :t:`simple path resolution` is determined as
1698: follows:
1699: 
1700: * :dp:`fls_mk0ufkeggot6`
1701:   If the :t:`simple path` is part of an :t:`attribute` or a
1702:   :t:`macro invocation`, then the :t:`namespace context` is the
1703:   :t:`macro namespace`.
1704: 
1705: * :dp:`fls_ayv8okec9fwb`
1706:   If the :t:`simple path` is part of a :t:`use import`, then the
1707:   :t:`namespace context` consists of the :t:`macro namespace`, the
1708:   :t:`type namespace`, and the :t:`value namespace`.
1709: 
1710: * :dp:`fls_ppoc6wcplab6`
1711:   If the :t:`simple path` is part of a :t:`visibility modifier`, then the
1712:   :t:`namespace context` consists of the :t:`type namespace`.
1713: 
1714: :dp:`fls_dc0yv4306p82`
1715: The leftmost :t:`path segment` of a :t:`simple path` is resolved using general
1716: :t:`path resolution`. The remaining :t:`[path segment]s` are resolved in
1717: left-to-right order, as follows:
1718: 
1719: * :dp:`fls_jhivcca0xcqj`
1720:   If the current :t:`path segment` is expressed as :t:`keyword` ``super``, then
1721:   what the current :t:`path segment` resolves to and its
1722:   :t:`resolution context` is the :t:`entity` of the parent :t:`module` of the
1723:   previous :t:`[path segment]'s` :t:`resolution context`.
1724: 
1725: * :dp:`fls_tfsgutcpube2`
1726:   Otherwise try to find a :t:`candidate selected entity` whose :t:`name`
1727:   matches the characters of the current :t:`path segment` within the previous
1728:   :t:`[path segment]'s` :t:`resolution context`, where if the current
1729:   :t:`path segment` is not the rightmost :t:`path segment`, the
1730:   :t:`resolution context` is restricted to the :t:`entities <entity>` of
1731:   :t:`[module]s`. What the current :t:`path segment` resolves to and its
1732:   :t:`resolution context` is that :t:`candidate selected entity`.
1733: 
1734: .. _fls_o9u2h5m17kpz:
1735: 
1736: Path Expression Resolution
1737: ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
1738: 
1739: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
1740: 
1741: :dp:`fls_akjlqm3a2lb1`
1742: :dt:`Path expression resolution` is a form of :t:`path resolution` that applies
1743: to a :t:`unqualified path expression`.
1744: 
1745: :dp:`fls_xyzdajtf4u2t`
1746: The :t:`namespace context` of :t:`path expression resolution` is the
1747: :t:`value namespace`.
1748: 
1749: :dp:`fls_d45vu3iazi3`
1750: The leftmost :t:`path segment` of a :t:`unqualified path expression` is resolved using
1751: general :t:`path resolution`. The remaining :t:`[path segment]s` are resolved
1752: in left-to-right order, as follows:
1753: 
1754: * :dp:`fls_9pjhok9rctty`
1755:   If the current :t:`path segment` is expressed as :t:`keyword` ``super``, then
1756:   what the current :t:`path segment` resolves to and its
1757:   :t:`resolution context` is the :t:`entity` of the parent :t:`module` of the
1758:   previous :t:`[path segment]'s` :t:`resolution context`.
1759: 
1760: * :dp:`fls_2wbpr9lvz5yq`
1761:   If the current :t:`path segment` is the rightmost :t:`path segment`, then
1762: 
1763:   * :dp:`fls_u5nyv6ii1g2l`
1764:     If the previous :t:`[path segment]'s` :t:`resolution context` is an
1765:     :t:`enum type`, then try to find a :t:`candidate selected entity` whose
1766:     :t:`name` matches the characters of the current :t:`path segment`, where
1767:     the :t:`resolution context` is restricted to the :t:`entities <entity>` of
1768:     the :t:`[enum variant]s` of that :t:`enum type`. What the current
1769:     :t:`path segment` resolves to and its :t:`resolution context` is that
1770:     :t:`candidate selected entity`.
1771: 
1772:   * :dp:`fls_bscg48os5otx`
1773:     If the previous :t:`[path segment]'s` :t:`resolution context` is a
1774:     :t:`module`, then try to find a :t:`candidate selected entity` whose
1775:     :t:`name` matches the characters of the current :t:`path segment` within
1776:     that :t:`module`. What the current :t:`path segment` resolves to and its
1777:     :t:`resolution context` is that :t:`candidate selected entity`.
1778: 
1779:   * :dp:`fls_qbmp0blpoxx9`
1780:     If the previous :t:`[path segment]'s` :t:`resolution context` is a
1781:     :t:`trait` or a :t:`type`, then perform
1782:     :t:`path expression resolution implementation candidate lookup` for the
1783:     current :t:`path segment` and that :t:`trait` or :t:`type`.
1784: 
1785: * :dp:`fls_ydni5laqv6gp`
1786:   Otherwise
1787: 
1788:   * :dp:`fls_lsxbl6ep3150`
1789:     If the previous :t:`[path segment]'s` :t:`resolution context` is a
1790:     :t:`module`, then try to find a :t:`candidate selected entity` whose
1791:     :t:`name` matches the characters of the current :t:`path segment` within
1792:     that :t:`module`, where the :t:`resolution context` is restricted to the
1793:     :t:`entities <entity>` whose :t:`[name]s` reside in the
1794:     :t:`type namespace`. What the current :t:`path segment` resolves to and its
1795:     :t:`resolution context` is that :t:`candidate selected entity`.
1796: 
1797:   * :dp:`fls_x1n7w8w6lwm`
1798:     If the previous :t:`[path segment]'s` :t:`resolution context` is a
1799:     :t:`trait`, then try to find a :t:`candidate selected entity` whose
1800:     :t:`name` matches the characters of the current :t:`path segment` within
1801:     that :t:`trait`, where the :t:`resolution context` is restricted to the
1802:     :t:`entities <entity>` of all :t:`[associated item]s` of that :t:`trait`.
1803:     What the current :t:`path segment` resolves to and its
1804:     :t:`resolution context` is that :t:`candidate selected entity`.
1805: 
1806:   * :dp:`fls_v1h4frnbqruu`
1807:     If the previous :t:`[path segment]'s` :t:`resolution context` is a
1808:     :t:`type`, then try to find a :t:`candidate selected entity` whose
1809:     :t:`name` matches the characters of the current :t:`path segment` within
1810:     that :t:`type`, where the :t:`resolution context` is restricted to the
1811:     :t:`entities <entity>` of all :t:`[associated item]s` from its
1812:     :t:`[inherent implementation]s`, and the :t:`entities <entity>` of all its
1813:     :t:`[trait implementation]s` of :t:`[trait]s` that are :t:`in scope`. What
1814:     the current :t:`path segment` resolves to and its :t:`resolution context`
1815:     is that :t:`candidate selected entity`.
1816: 
1817: :dp:`fls_utfpnwlo0v99`
1818: :dt:`Path expression resolution implementation candidate lookup` for a
1819: :t:`path segment` and a :t:`trait` or :t:`type` proceeds as follows:
1820: 
1821: #. :dp:`fls_1p8ocf1w5bp4`
1822:    Perform
1823:    :t:`path expression resolution inherent implementation candidate lookup` for
1824:    the :t:`path segment` and the :t:`trait` or :t:`type`.
1825: 
1826: #. :dp:`fls_qb5yo7j5gnvf`
1827:    Perform
1828:    :t:`path expression resolution trait implementation candidate lookup` for
1829:    the :t:`path segment` and the :t:`trait` or :t:`type`.
1830: 
1831: :dp:`fls_o1g0forw6xw`
1832: :dt:`Path expression resolution inherent implementation candidate lookup` for a
1833: :t:`path segment` and a :t:`trait` or :t:`type` proceeds as follows:
1834: 
1835: #. :dp:`fls_bcqe13q696zg`
1836:    For each :t:`inherent implementation` in the set of
1837:    :t:`[inherent implementation]s` of the :t:`trait` or :t:`type` where the
1838:    :t:`implementing type` :t:`unifies <unify>` with the :t:`trait` or :t:`type`
1839: 
1840:    #. :dp:`fls_3sceutaqpqha`
1841:       Try to locate a visible :t:`constant` or a visible :t:`function` in the
1842:       :t:`inherent implementation` whose :t:`name` matches the characters of
1843:       the :t:`path segment`.
1844: 
1845:    #. :dp:`fls_6q9cwqlvxmd1`
1846:       If such a :t:`constant` or :t:`function` exists, then the
1847:       :t:`path segment` resolves to that :t:`constant` or :t:`function` and
1848:       :t:`path expression resolution` stops.
1849:

8: Associated Items
9: ================
10: 
11: .. rubric:: Syntax
12: 
13: .. syntax::
14: 
15:    AssociatedItem ::=
16:        OuterAttributeOrDoc* (AssociatedItemWithVisibility | TerminatedMacroInvocation)
17: 
18:    AssociatedItemWithVisibility ::=
19:        VisibilityModifier? (
20:            ConstantDeclaration
21:          | FunctionDeclaration
22:          | TypeAliasDeclaration
23:        )
24: 
25: .. rubric:: Legality Rules
26: 
27: :dp:`fls_ckzd25qd213t`
28: An :dt:`associated item` is an :t:`item` that appears within an
29: :t:`implementation` or a :t:`trait`.
30: 
31: :dp:`fls_8cz4rdrklaj4`
32: An :t:`associated type` is a :t:`type alias` that appears as an
33: :t:`associated item`.
34: 
35: :dp:`fls_wb049assocfn`
36: An :dt:`associated function` is a :t:`function` that appears as an
37: :t:`associated item`.
38: 
39: :dp:`fls_nNisRZ6p4T6u`
40: An :dt:`incomplete associated constant` is an :t:`associated constant` without
41: a :t:`constant initializer`.
42: 
43: :dp:`fls_nMEacKMoaVn0`
44: An :dt:`incomplete associated function` is an :t:`associated function` without
45: a :t:`function body`.
46: 
47: :dp:`fls_FJEqMV7Qt6ZJ`
48: An :dt:`incomplete associated type` is an :t:`associated type` without an
49: :t:`initialization type`.
50: 
51: :dp:`fls_w8nu8suy7t5`
52: An :t:`associated type` shall not be used in the :t:`path expression` of a
53: :t:`struct expression`.
54: 
55: :dp:`fls_wasocqdnuzd1`
56: An :t:`associated type` with a :s:`TypeBoundList` shall appear only as an
57: :t:`associated trait type`.
58: 
59: :dp:`fls_PeD0DzjK57be`
60: A :dt:`generic associated type` is an :t:`associated type` with
61: :t:`[generic parameter]s`.
62: 
63: :dp:`fls_3foYUch29ZtF`
64: A :t:`lifetime parameter` of a :t:`generic associated type` requires a
65: :t:`bound` of the form ``T: 'lifetime``, where ``T`` is a :t:`type parameter`
66: or :c:`Self` and ``'lifetime`` is the :t:`lifetime parameter`, when
67: 
68: * :dp:`fls_SnQc0zZS57Cz`
69:   The :t:`generic associated type` is used in an :t:`associated function` of
70:   the same :t:`trait`, and
71: 
72: * :dp:`fls_6Z05BK2JSzpP`
73:   The corresponding :t:`lifetime argument` in the use is not the ``'static``
74:   :t:`lifetime` and has either an explicit :t:`bound` or an :t:`implied bound`
75:   that constrains the :t:`type parameter`, and
76: 
77: * :dp:`fls_AtItgS1UvwiX`
78:   The intersection of all such uses is not empty.
79: 
80: :dp:`fls_4ftfefcotb4g`
81: An :t:`associated implementation constant` shall have a :t:`constant
82: initializer`.
83: 
84: :dp:`fls_qb5qpfe0uwk`
85: An :dt:`associated implementation function` is an :t:`associated function` that
